speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do have one reaction to this, as you stabbed and missed, you have a reaction as well, after you've taken it. You pull yourself from under its fist, and it's now starting to get up onto its feet proper. Oh! What you got?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Presumably, he doesn't seem to notice you there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: -- disappeared-ed-ed-ed. Yeah. So I'd like to stab him in the back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make an attack roll on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hey, you're wearing a cool shirt.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did you roll the meteorite yet?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I hope you guys got my word that you did not try to capture him, did you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, we didn't. And everything's gone horribly wrong because of that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, don't try to-- No, don't capture him, that's a terrible idea!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're already in the giant battle.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If you had been here five minutes earlier, you might have helped shape what happened.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 18? Plus eight, that's 26! All right, versus its athletics. Okay. So as it's getting up, suddenly you freak out, bring your hand up, and you summon that same sparkling wave of arcane energy that shoots out from your hands. Forms this giant version of your hand. Clings around both his arms, his torso, and tightens itself around its body. As it does, it stands up, and it's currently grappled.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Bigby's Hand. Grab him. Grapple him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I can grapple a massive beast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, an ogre comes up to his waist.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It can grapple a Huge or smaller creature.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so. So go ahead and make a roll, a contested roll for that, if you don't mind. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You got this, you got this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're going to find out here in a second, let me pull up the spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I got it right here. Concentration--- No, AC. The hand has an AC of--- No, here it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so. I got it right here. Grasping Hand, all right. So use the hand's strength score to resolve the grapple. So you roll that and add eight to it. What'd you roll on the d20?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That's okay. It'll take you guys about two rounds to get there. At a full round. So--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not very much, nine points total.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nine points of damage total to it. All right, gotcha. To this wondrous entity. Yay! All right. All right, great. So that ends your turn. It is currently grappled.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I jump away as a reaction?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. Go ahead, and we'll say you've been down here, Scanlan is right here, and you are right here. Not with Trinket, because you went out to scout and stealth. So that's you there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I hit my earring up, and go  Giant! Giant! Being attacked! Help!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys hear that, those of you who have earrings. Shout that out? The rest of you are starting to run.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At full-round sprints, yeah. It's not going to be a very quiet walk for Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hey, I don't need to be stealthy, now!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That would have been really cool. Unfortunately, he still has a lot of hit points left. Sleep arrow is something to glide into later in the battle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not damage. This is just to see whether or not you can put it to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he's got to have a--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's 12, okay. 16, 28. And then I get another--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've got access to this thing. Let me show you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Math is happening here. Action Math!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, the arrow pierces him and he pulls it out, and you can see for a second the eye gets a little--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He flicks the arrow off and it snaps as it leaves his fingers, and he glares down at you with his other eye, angrily. Shrugs off the effect.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Man! That would have been-- that's impossible!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He looks-- There's been a lot of grappling this past round. He took a lot of damage before everyone else showed up and it's been just a lot of holding him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to start constricting.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Grog, you might have to talk to him again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so go ahead and roll a d20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if I can.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's the strength bonus of the form?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I get a plus six. Oh wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's it say for the constricting? It should have the rule there for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you add six to that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You start moving around and even though he's still struggling in his form, he gets one hand up and is holding you at bay. You have not managed to grapple him yet, but you are in the process of trying to. Next round you can try again. He's pretty strong.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: When are you clever folk going to get in his head?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I am going to-- Has he taken a lot of damage yet, or are we just keep grappling him?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "For eight hours, no. Nothing in my repertoire lasts that long, which is why I say let me take his mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Until it resists, that's no good.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Could you perhaps put him to sleep for the evening?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Or can we figure out a way to restrain him?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, can we quickly tie him down while we're talking?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Because here's the thing, I have an ability where I can use Geas. And cast Geas on him, and I basically give him a command and he'll essentially become our big--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He'll be our big, beefy minion for 30 days.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And if he resists it, he takes psychic damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We have have manacles, and we have chains.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Put him in the Bag of Holding!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's frickin' tie him down. At least can we tie him up right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: We'll secure him, but Clarota, could you prolong the Sleep spell for eight hours so we can rest and you could do your spell?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I do not know. I was not around when it was created. I didn't know it existed until you saw it. I have not been in the city in months. Nearly a year."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't quite work that way. It works on creatures. I'm not sure I could do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm full of those, but let's hear yours.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What if we break the fall of the device with Clarota?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm saying, what if we lure it into the center of the room and then drop the ceiling on top of it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Clarota is currently stepping back with a look of confusion.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm a bit tired. I'm so sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Your name is confusing to us. This is why we wanted to call you Clarence. Sorry, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the term 'Clarence', you see the tentacles go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he's warming up to it. I think he's liking it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Clarota, how much does the crystal weigh?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You can see humanoid-sized individuals gliding through the city. 20, 30, 50 illithid visible in one section of the city. Each carrying two to three slaves each. Some trolls, ogres, other dwarves, other creatures of the Underdark that at this distance you cannot make out their form, but there is, essentially, a thriving community down here of mind flayers. Some of them, it's hard to tell where they're going but there are some going to and from the temple carrying small satchels or boxes. As they glide-- Or, actually, they wouldn't be carrying it. Their slaves would be carrying for them and they're keeping alongside them. As you continue around the side here, you start getting more into the ruined area and this part of the town itself, on this side, is mostly nonfunctional. It's abandoned and you can see the repurposed city construction by this colony has started in the center and over the past few hundred years or as long as it's been here, it's slowly building and repairing outward. As you crest around this side, you can see a far ruin about another half mile to a mile off from where you are, too far away to catch any details of it. You cruise around this side, and you hear this squeaking sound, this (squeaks), and you glance up, and you see what looks like a cluster of bats moving to the top of the cavern. They're almost buzzing you guys in the head, up above, ten, 20 feet ahead of you, they're just coming back and forth over you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Both of you roll stealth. With advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 14, 18. Okay. So, you guys begin to crest around, and you look down and it's very, very dark. There are what looks like these greenish spores that found pockets in the city that give off light. This very, very faint luminescence within the city, and they get thicker and thicker and cluster and make the center of the city more bright and easier to make out the details of. You don't know if these were placed there or if they naturally grew there and just add the ambiance to it. But what you see in that little bit of light as you glance down are many figures walking and moving through the city.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I'd say it'd take about 20 minutes or so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Or we're casting Fly on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hold on a tick. How much can you lift, Grog?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I weight 645 pounds, so a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog is not strong enough to carry a bear and you on a Fly spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What if we cast Fly on the bear?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: No no no. I mean one at a time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can try. There's a good chance he might drop Trinket on the way over. From that height, which would be a splatter bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can you cast Fly on Trinket and then have Grog ride Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What if one of us ride Trinket?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, but Grog won't fit on the flying carpet. This is a really-- This is like a mind puzzle.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How long does it take to make the trip?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From flying over there on the carpet, at its full speed--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Scanlan and I flew over there.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, it's not concentration. As many fourth-level spell as you have, you can cast it on different people. It lasts for eight hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll cast Inspiration on this gentleman for his throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so that's a d10. You can do that up to four people.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Either an attack, a saving throw-- I'd recommend saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's an inspiration dice to you and--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A d10. And Vax, Vax, a d10 inspiration to you as well. Keep track of those guys. These are the only ones you get from the bard this game, because he used them up front. Buff spells that want to be cast at this point in time?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am clicking my Boots of Haste.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (sings) And so I come to you with Inspiration.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike, any buff spells you wanna put on?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I am gonna cast Death Ward on Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, is Grog the only one you want to cast it on?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I can cast it on more?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Since the rays automatically hit, I'll consider that those who get hit by the rays will have advantage on their saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I will hold off on that-- Okay so, I will, just-- I'm going to do a Targeted head shot. I'm going to grab the rope with my bonus action--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes. I'm pulling out the Bad News.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to take a shot-- Basically take a Targeted head shot at him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. So, this does a couple things. Where's my-- I know. Where the hell are my--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Guys, come on. This is like, piece of cake, there's one guy. There's like eight of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The poison has worn off. K'Varn is still bleeding, currently. So, how many damage for that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's only ten points of damage, but the nice thing that that does, which is that, for the rest of his turn, up until the end of his turn, he has disadvantage on all attacks.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 19, okay, you manage to reduce the damage from the fall somewhat. You suffer 11 points of damage. Managing to land harshly on one knee and foot. You feel that's going to suck in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Three rays on its turn and then it gets to make three individual ray attacks on other people's turns.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's still, technically speaking though, we would still have advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: May I use my Cutting Words?

preceding_turns.text: Unfortunately, it isn't an attack roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's not an attack, okay. Then a strength saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, With advantage. Oh good. Ee. Not good. Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So a familiar beam streaks up, kind of a deep green color slams into you. And you feel yourself chucked off the top, restrained, and then pulled down with severe force into the center of the temple.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, into the room? But I don't want to be in there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And then the beam releases you. You go slamming into the ground. Go ahead and make an acrobatics roll. You have advantage because of your dexterity.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. It's now Kima's turn. Kima's going to hold off, looks over at Keyleth and says, "If you can give me another one of those tosses like you gave Grog?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he can see me? I'm in his field of vision?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'll move out of the cone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And now I try to blind him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, I-- Through the Hand Cone of Clarity, of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That is a 20. What is your DC?

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: (sings) Wrapped up like a douche, another roller in the night.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What's the DC of your spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you release the spell, the eyestalks turn and you can see the darkness that normally fades over the eyes just gloss over and dissipate.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Great. Well then, as my bonus action, I'll give Grog just a little bit more healing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Put it this way. Since you stood up, you're now like cusping in the cone-- The anti-magic cone. Any potions drank in that area will have no effect. Magic, the actual potions, are dispelled.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But is happy with the damage that she dealt. Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. That's your movement to get up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's my movement to get up?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: God, son of a bitch. He's looking at me, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's kind of looking in that direction. Not directly down at you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But I'm in the cone of shit?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're just on the cusp of it, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I use the Chain of Returning to throw my axe at him?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, 'cause I can't move anywhere, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think-- Can I use it-- Can I heal myself after I throw it? Can I? Or is it one or the other?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: We have Schmoozer, donated $25. "Another 25 to you guys to correct myself for last week. I'm slowly falling more and more in love with you guys. Since the Overlord says my original message implied I wasn't in love at the start, but I surely was." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: All right. By the way, do you guys want to know how many shirts we sold?

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you guys went through it, man. Right through it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know, we're all so jittery.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is where you need a neighborhood bar. Where we can go and, like-- Go, just, what the fuck just happened?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I just gained the ability to do those head shots, like, two games ago, too. I'm just so excited.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: SalazarJack donated 100 bucks. "Thanks everyone for your time and all the effort you put into Critical Role." Very appreciated. Thank you. Sepherie, 100 bucks. "I introduced my college roommate and best friend to play D&D. 15 years later, we've lost touch. Found out we both watch this show. We watch most Thursdays now. Thanks for being so--" (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Dope-ass effect." There we go. My brain and eyes. Ugh. GrimProspect, ten bucks. "Absolutely love you guys. Had a pretty awful day but hearing Travis do his amazing Roy Mustang voice could make it all better."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Five bucks. "Ashley, as an actual rocket scientist that will never get into space. I just wanted to say that your dream is my dream."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Inspire us all and never stop reaching for the stars."

speaker: MATT
raw_text: ZandorInvictus donated 25 bucks. "Had a blast tonight as usual and also had a blast at my first game in seven years. I thank you. My copper dragonborn fighter Restule Othor thanks you and my half-elf bard Cedric Riverbend thanks you. LessThanThree." AbowFard donated 25 bucks. "Happy to donate to a good cause. Thanks for the great entertainment each week. I look forward to many of your adventures and also playing 5e D&D with my kids." Arandis donated 25 bucks. "Awesome job with the show as always. Donating in the hopes it inspires you in your upcoming battles as you help others battle lack of education and illiteracy in the real world." That's awesome. Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: SalazarJack donated 100 bucks. "Thanks everyone for your time and all the effort you put into Critical Role." Very appreciated. Thank you. Sepherie, 100 bucks. "I introduced my college roommate and best friend to play D&D. 15 years later, we've lost touch. Found out we both watch this show. We watch most Thursdays now. Thanks for being so--" (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: JoshPotato66 donated five bucks. "I love this show. It's like great theatre. Can't wait to see how far this thing goes and can't wait to see some D&D at some point in the near future. P.S. I have to admit I have a little crush on Pike, Vex, and Keyleth."

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Ameliar, 20 bucks. "You guys provide an awesome show each week. Since week one, thanks for keep staying awesome for us Critters." CritterJody, ten bucks. "I struggle with depression and anxiety and the past few days have been extremely bad for me. This show brought me out of it. Thank you." Dright'sFury donated 20 bucks. "Awesome job, kick-starting my imagination. Love love love this stream." Thank you, bud. HighlyAmusing donated 25 bucks. "Thanks for putting such an awesome show on and making the middle of my work week a little more bearable. Love you guys. Also, made a matching donation to 826 here in Chicago, too." Aww.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep, "And I wanted to not only thank you but donate as well. Hopefully I'll get a chance to meet the rest of you soon." Dractheon donated $10. "Here's to Critical Role, my now-favorite part of the week. Hearing all these amazing characters has finally moved toward my dream of being a voice actor. On a side note, Liam, can you do a few lines of Illidan for me? I'm prepared for it."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (Illidan voice) You are not preared! You stay up and watch livestream into the middle of the night. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Illidan's been drinking. Baccicio, I think. Sorry. Baccicio, there you go. $5. "I love watching your show. You guys are awesome. Can't wait to start my own D&D group. I'm already working on my own half-elf druid. Hope this helps 826LA." (cheering)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Kieris, 10 bucks. "We wanted to play D&D for years and due to Critical Role I started to DM for my online friends who had never played. We used Tabletop Simulator. Played for the past few days and having a blast. Thank you so much for your inspiration, humor and awesomeness."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: LoneWolfAvesty, 50 bucks. "Thank you all for making the best 3+ hours of each week for me. I'm either sitting on the edge of my seat or laughing out loud for reals. Love you all."
